hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَی قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ع یَقُولُ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنِ اطَّلَی وَ اخْتَضَبَ بِالْحِنَّاءِ آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ ثَلَاثِ خِصَالٍ الْجُذَامِ وَ الْبَرَصِ وَ الْآکِلَهِ إِلَی طَلْیَهٍ مِثْلِهَا.
hadiths.en: 6.  It is narrated from Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who applies Noorah and dyes his body with henna Allah would keep him away from three things  till  he  applies  them  again.  Those  three  things  are diseases like leprosy, leucoderma and scabies.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 44, H. 81
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of dyeing one’s hair
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٤، ح ٨١

hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ جَدِّهِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع النُّورَهُ نُشْرَهٌ وَ طَهُورٌ لِلْجَسَدِ.
hadiths.en: 1.  It  is  narrated  from  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  that  Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said, “Noorah is an amulet and it cleans and purifies the body.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 45, H. 82
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of applying Noorah
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٥، ح ٨٢

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ نَصْرِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَنْبَسَهَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ رَفَعَ الْحَدِیثَ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص تَسْرِیحُ الرَّأْسِ یَذْهَبُ بِالْوَبَاءِ وَ یَجْلِبُ الرِّزْقَ وَ یَزِیدُ فِی الْجِمَاعِ.
hadiths.en: 1.  The  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  says,  “Combing  one’s  hair keeps away plague, increases earnings and increases sexual power.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 46, H. 83
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of combing one’s hair
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٦، ح ٨٣

hadiths.chapter: 47. Reward of combing one’s beard seventy times
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُمَرَ الْهَمْدَانِیِّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَطِیَّهَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ: مَنْ سَرَّحَ لِحْیَتَهُ سَبْعِینَ مَرَّهً وَ عَدَّهَا مَرَّهً مَرَّهً لَمْ یَقْرَبْهُ الشَّیْطَانُ أَرْبَعِینَ صَبَاحاً.
hadiths.en: 1. It was narrated to me by al-Hussain b. Ahmad from Sahl b. Ziyad from Ibrahim b. Abdulrahman b. al-Hajjaj from Muhammad b. Umar al-Hamadani from Ibn ’Atiyyah from Ismail b. Jabir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said who said: “Shaitaan will remain aloof for next forty days from one who combs his beard seventy times.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 47, H. 84
reference.chapter: Reward of combing one’s beard seventy times
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of combing one’s beard seventy times
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٧، ح ٨٤

hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ یَعْقُوبَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: الْإِثْمِدُ یَجْلُو الْبَصَرَ وَ یَقْطَعُ الدَّمْعَهَ وَ یُنْبِتُ الشَّعْرَ.
hadiths.en: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from al-Hussain b. Ali b. Fadhal from Ali b. ’Uqba from Yunis b. Ya’qub from some of our companions from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Surma improves eyesight, stops tears and helps hair growth.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 48, H. 85
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of applying Antimony (Surma)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٨، ح ٨٥

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُقَاتِلٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ: مَنْ کانَ یُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَ الْیَوْمِ الْآخِرِ فَلْیَکْتَحِلْ.
hadiths.en: 2. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Ali from his father from Ali b. Muhammad from Abdillah b. Muqatil from Abi al-Hassan al-Ridha (a.s.) who said: “A person who has faith on Allah and the Day of Judgment would surely apply Antimony.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 48, H. 86
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of applying Antimony (Surma)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٨، ح ٨٦

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ حَمْزَهَ بْنِ بَزِیعٍ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: الْکُحْلُ عِنْدَ النَّوْمِ أَمَانٌ مِنَ الْمَاءِ.
hadiths.en: 3. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. Ja’far from Musa b. ’Umayra from Hamzah b. Bazee’ from Ishaq b. ’Amir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Applying Antimony before going to bed stops watering of the eyes.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 48, H. 87
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of applying Antimony (Surma)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٨، ح ٨٧

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی الْحَسَنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: الْکُحْلُ یُنْبِتُ الشَّعْرَ وَ یُجَفِّفُ الدَّمْعَهَ وَ یُعْذِبُ الرِّیقَ وَ یَجْلُو الْبَصَرَ.
hadiths.en: 4. It was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Ahmad from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Sahl b. Ziyad from Ibn Sinan from Hamad b. Uthman from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Applying Antimony helps hair growth, tears dry up, spit thickens and eyesight improves.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 48, H. 88
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of applying Antimony (Surma)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٨، ح ٨٨

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع اسْتَأْصِلْ شَعْرَکَ تَقِلَّ دَوَابُّهُ وَ دَرَنُهُ وَ وَسَخُهُ وَ تَغْلُظْ رَقَبَتُکَ وَ یَجْلُو بَصَرَکَ.
hadiths.en: 1.  The narrator says that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said to him, “Cut  your  hair  so  that  dandruff  and  lice  gets  reduced. Cutting hair broadens one’s neck and improves eyesight.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 49, H. 89
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of cutting one’s hair
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤٩، ح ٨٩

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ زَیْدٍ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَلَّمَ أَظْفَارَهُ یَوْمَ الْجُمُعَهِ أَخْرَجَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ أَنَامِلِهِ الدَّاءَ وَ أَدْخَلَ فِیهَا الدَّوَاءَ.
hadiths.en: 1.  It  is  narrated  by  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  from  his predecessors that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said, “For one who trims his nails on Friday the Almighty Allah would remove pain from his fingers and insert medicine to them.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 90
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٠

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَلَّمَ أَظْفَارَهُ یَوْمَ السَّبْتِ أَوْ یَوْمَ الْخَمِیسِ وَ أَخَذَ مِنْ شَارِبِهِ عُوفِیَ مِنْ وَجَعِ الْأَضْرَاسِ وَ وَجَعِ الْعَیْنِ.
hadiths.en: 2.  The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) says, “One who trims his nails and  moustache  on  Thursday  or  Saturday  will  remain  safe from the pain of teeth and eyes.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 91
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩١

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الرَّازِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ زَکَرِیَّا عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ یَحْیَی قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَصَّ أَظَافِیرَهُ یَوْمَ الْخَمِیسِ وَ تَرَکَ وَاحِدَهً لِیَوْمِ الْجُمُعَهِ نَفَی اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَنْهُ الْفَقْرَ.
hadiths.en: 3.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “One who trims all his nails except  one  on  Thursday  and  trims  the  remaining  one  on Friday, Allah would remove his poverty.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 92
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٢

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ جَدِّهِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص تَقْلِیمُ الْأَظْفَارِ یَمْنَعُ الدَّاءَ الْأَعْظَمَ وَ یَزِیدُ فِی الرِّزْقِ.
hadiths.en: 4.  It  is  narrated  by  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  from  his predecessors   that   the   Holy   Prophet   (s.a.w.s.)   said, “Trimming  nails  prevents  serious  hardships  and  increases one’s earnings.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 93
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٣

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ الْمَدَائِنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: تَقْلِیمُ الْأَظْفَارِ یَوْمَ الْجُمُعَهِ یُؤْمِنُ مِنَ الْجُذَامِ وَ الْبَرَصِ وَ الْعَمَی فَإِنْ لَمْ تَحْتَجْ فَحُکَّهَا حَکّاً.
hadiths.en: 5. And from the previous chain from Muhammad b. Isa from Abi Ayoob’ al-Madaini from Abi ’Umayra from Hisham b. Salim from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Trimming nails on Friday saves one from leprosy, leucoderma and blindness. If it is not possible to trim them, one should at least rub them on something.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 94
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٤

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: وَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَلَّمَ أَظْفَارَهُ وَ قَصَّ شَارِبَهُ فِی کُلِّ جُمُعَهٍ- ثُمَّ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَ بِاللَّهِ وَ عَلَی مِلَّهِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص أُعْطِیَ بِکُلِّ قُلَامَهٍ عِتْقَ رَقَبَهٍ مِنْ وُلْدِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ.
hadiths.en: 6.  Imam   Ja’far   Sadiq   (a.s.)   says,   “One   who   recites Bismillaahi wa billaahi  wa a’laa millaati rasoolillaah while trimming  nails  and  moustache  on  Friday  would  get  the reward of freeing a slave from the progeny of Ismail (a.s.) for each hair and nail trimmed.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 95
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٥

hadiths.chapter: 50. Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ أَبِی کَهْمَسٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَلِّمْنِی دُعَاءً أَسْتَنْزِلُ بِهِ الرِّزْقَ فَقَالَ لِی خُذْ مِنْ شَارِبِکَ وَ أَظْفَارِکَ وَ لْیَکُنْ ذَلِکَ فِی یَوْمِ الْجُمُعَهِ. قال محمد بن علی مؤلف هذا الکتاب قال أبی رض فی وصیه إلی قلم أظفارک و خذ من شاربک و ابدأ بخنصرک من یدک الیمنی و قل حین ترید تقلمها أو جز شاربک بسم الله و بالله و علی مله رسول الله ص فإنه من فعل ذلک کتب الله له بکل قلامه و جزازه عتق نسمه و لم یمرض إلا مرضه الذی یموت فیه.
hadiths.en: 7. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Salih b. ’Uqba from Abi Kahmas who said: “One of the advices of my father, may Allah’s peace be upon him, was: ‘O son! Trim your nails and moustache. Start trimming nails from the small finger of right hand and whenever you do the above two deeds, recite this supplication Bismillaahi wa billaahi wa a’laa millati rasoolillaah. O son! To one who performs this deed Allah would grant the reward of releasing a slave for each nail and hair trimmed and he will not be involved in any disease except death.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 50, H. 96
reference.chapter: Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of trimming one’s nails and moustache
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٠، ح ٩٦

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ السَّیَّارِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی سُلَیْمَانَ الْخَوَّاصِ عَنْ أَبِی نُعَیْمٍ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ دُکَیْنٍ عَنْ سَدِیرٍ الصَّیْرَفِیِّ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع وَ عَلَیَّ نَعْلٌ بَیْضَاءُ فَقَالَ یَا سَدِیرُ مَا هَذِهِ النَّعْلُ أَخَذْتَهَا عَلَی عِلْمٍ فَقُلْتُ لَا وَ اللَّهِ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاکَ فَقَالَ مَنْ دَخَلَ السُّوقَ قَاصِداً لِشِرَاءِ نَعْلٍ بَیْضَاءَ لَمْ یُبْلِهَا حَتَّی یَکْتَسِبَ مَالًا مِنْ حَیْثُ لا یَحْتَسِبُ قَالَ وَ أَخْبَرَنِی أَبُو نُعَیْمٍ أَنَّ سَدِیراً أَخْبَرَ أَنَّهُ لَمْ یُبْلِ تِلْکَ النَّعْلَ حَتَّی اکْتَسَبَ مِائَهَ دِینَارٍ مِنْ حَیْثُ لَمْ یَحْتَسِبْ.
hadiths.en: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan (rah) who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Sayyari from Abi Sulayman al-Khawwadh from Abi Na’im al-Fadhl b. Dukayn from Sadeer al-Sayrafi who said: “One who goes to market with an intention of buying white shoes and buys them; then before those shoes are worn out, that person gets such a wealth, which he would have never expected.” Abu Naeem says that Sudair informed him saying, “My shoes were not worn out yet and I received a hundred dirhams from somewhere I had never expected.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 51, H. 97
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of wearing white shoes
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥١، ح ٩٧

hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَبَلَهَ عَنْ حَنَانِ بْنِ سَدِیرٍ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع وَ عَلَیَّ نَعْلٌ سَوْدَاءُ فَقَالَ مَا لَکَ وَ لُبْسَ نَعْلٍ سَوْدَاءَ أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَ فِیهَا ثَلَاثَ خِصَالٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ مَا هِیَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاکَ فَقَالَ تُضَعِّفُ الْبَصَرَ وَ تُرْخِی الذَّکَرَ وَ تُورِثُ الْهَمَّ وَ هِیَ مَعَ ذَلِکَ لِبَاسُ الْجَبَّارِینَ عَلَیْکَ بِلُبْسِ النَّعْلِ الصَّفْرَاءِ فَإِنَّ فِیهَا ثَلَاثَ خِصَالٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ مَا هِیَ قَالَ تَحُدُّ الْبَصَرَ وَ تَشُدُّ الذَّکَرَ وَ تَنْفِی الْهَمَّ وَ هِیَ مَعَ ذَلِکَ لِبَاسُ الْأَنْبِیَاءِ.
hadiths.en: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. ’Umayr from Abdillah b. Jabalah from Hanan b. Sadeer who said: “Why are you wearing black shoes? Do you know its bad consequences?” He asked, “What are its bad effects?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “It affects eyesight and male prowess and it is a cause of sadness and anger. Further, it is the sign of oppressors. Wear yellow shoes and enjoy its benefits.” He asked, “What are the benefits of wearing them?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “It improves eyesight and increases male prowess. It ends sadness and anger and it is a part of the dress of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.).”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 52, H. 98
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of wearing yellow shoes
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٢، ح ٩٨

hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَبَلَهَ عَنْ أَبِی الْجَارُودِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: لُبْسُ الْخُفِّ یَزِیدُ فِی قُوَّهِ الْبَصَرِ.
hadiths.en: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan (rah) who said it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Abdillah b. Jabalah from Abi al-Jarud from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Wearing shoes improves eyesight.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 53, H. 99
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of wearing shoes
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٣، ح ٩٩

hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ عِمْرَانَ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِدْمَانُ لُبْسِ الْخُفِّ أَمَانٌ مِنَ الْجُذَامِ قَالَ قُلْتُ فِی الشِّتَاءِ أَمْ فِی الصَّیْفِ قَالَ شِتَاءً کَانَ أَوْ صَیْفاً.
hadiths.en: 2. And from the previous chain from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. ’Imran from Ibn Mahbub from Muawiyah b. Wahab from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A person who wears shoes often does not get involved in leprosy.” The narrator asked, “Are you talking about summer or winter?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “There is no difference between them.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 53, H. 100
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of wearing shoes
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٥٣، ح ١٠٠
